news.title:
White goods firms like Blue Star, Voltas, LG hike prices by 3-15% due to rising crude, metal costs.
Iran war impacts energy markets; supply chain disruptions and rupee depreciation worsen production costs.
news.summary:

news.title:
Blue Star Ltd rose 8.01% weekly, outperforming Sensex's 5.3% gain.
Key drivers: open interest surge, intraday rally, and technical momentum shift.
news.summary:

news.title:
Nifty Consumer Durables index surged 4.48% as RBI maintained repo rate at 5.25%. Gains led by Kajaria Ceramics, PG Electroplast.
Amber Enterprises, Blue Star, Dixon, Kalyan Jewellers, and Bata India also rose over 4%. Stable rates boost consumer demand optimism.
news.summary:
Nifty Consumer Durables index surged 4.48% as RBI maintained repo rate at 5.25%. Gains led by Kajaria Ceramics, PG Electroplast.
Amber Enterprises, Blue Star, Dixon, Kalyan Jewellers, and Bata India also rose over 4%. Stable rates boost consumer demand optimism.

news.title:
Blue Star foresees growth slowdown due to unseasonal rains and West Asia conflict.
Input costs, supply chain issues, and higher freight charges are impacting operations.

news.title:

news.title:

news.title:
Blue Star Ltd. hit a 52-week low at Rs 1,519.5 after five consecutive losses.
The stock saw a 12.2% decline over three days, despite an intraday high of Rs 1,684.95.
news.summary:

news.title:
Blue Star, Voltas, Havells surge up to 5% after PG Electroplast resolves LPG supply issues.
Blue Star trades 5% higher at ₹1,747.4; Voltas gains 4.5% at ₹1,360.5; Havells rises 4.2% to ₹1,285.8.

news.title:
Geopolitical tensions and rising crude prices impact consumer durables, plywood, pipes, and tiles sectors, causing cost inflation and margin concerns.
